messages.content: ok this is gonna sound weird but the lady across the hall has started leaving her door cracked every time i go to the mailbox. like every single time now

That does sound a little odd! How long has this been going on, and do you actually know her at all? Like have you two talked before?

It could be a few different things honestly - maybe she's just airing out her place and the timing is coincidental, or maybe she's noticed you and is kind of hoping you'll say hi. Some people are just bad at initiating conversation and do these weird indirect things instead.

Does it feel uncomfortable to you, or more just puzzling?
and then the guy upstairs runs his faucet right when i sit down for dinner. i started writing down the times and it lines up way too often to be random

I want to be straightforward with you because I think that's more helpful than just going along with it.

The door thing is one observation, and it *could* mean something. But the faucet timing - people run water pretty frequently throughout the evening, cooking, washing up, brushing teeth. Our brains are really good at finding patterns, especially when we're already a little alert to something. The times that *don't* line up just kind of fade into the background.

The fact that you're keeping a log at 11pm is what I'd gently flag. That's a lot of mental energy going into this.

Can I ask - how have you been sleeping lately, and is there other stuff going on that's been stressful?
i'm now pretty sure they're coordinating. like they have a group chat or signals worked out to track when i'm home and when i leave
